questions.stimulus_text:
A rival manufacturer recently announced similar plans to introduce a mechanical pencil that would accept only the leads produced by that manufacturer.

In extensive test marketing, mechanical-pencil users found the new Write Company pencil markedly superior to other mechanical pencils they had used.
questions.question_text: Which of the following, if true, most strongly supports the bank officials' prediction?

options.a: Some of Enterprise Bank's current checking account customers are expected to switch to the new accounts once they are offered.
options.b: One-third of Enterprise Bank's revenues are currently derived from monthly fees tied to checking accounts.
options.c: Many checking account customers who occasionally pay a fee for not maintaining a minimum balance in their account generally maintain a balance well above the minimum.
options.d: Customers whose checking accounts do not have a minimum-balance requirement are more likely than others to overdraw their checking accounts.
options.e: Customers whose checking accounts do not have a minimum-balance requirement are more likely than others to write checks for small amounts.

questions.stimulus_text: Enterprise Bank currently requires customers with checking accounts to maintain a minimum balance or pay a monthly fee. Enterprise plans to offer accounts with no monthly fee and no minimum-balance requirement; to cover their projected administrative costs of $3 per account per month, they plan to charge $30 for overdrawing an account. Since each month on average slightly more than 10 percent of Enterprise's customers overdraw their accounts, bank officials predict the new accounts will generate a profit.
questions.question_text: Which of the following, if true, most strongly supports the bank officials' prediction?

options.a: Some of Enterprise Bank's current checking account customers are expected to switch to the new accounts once they are offered.
options.b: One-third of Enterprise Bank's revenues are currently derived from monthly fees tied to checking accounts.
options.c: Many checking account customers who occasionally pay a fee for not maintaining a minimum balance in their account generally maintain a balance well above the minimum.
options.d: Customers whose checking accounts do not have a minimum-balance requirement are more likely than others to overdraw their checking accounts.
options.e: Customers whose checking accounts do not have a minimum-balance requirement are more likely than others to write checks for small amounts.

questions.stimulus_text:
Highway official: When resurfacing our concrete bridges, we should use electrically conductive concrete (ECC) rather than standard concrete. In the winter, ECC can be heated by passing an electric current through it, thereby preventing ice buildup. The cost of the electricity needed is substantially lower than the cost of the deicing salt we currently use.

Taxpayer: But ECC is vastly more expensive than standard concrete, so your proposal is probably not justifiable on economic grounds.
questions.question_text: Which of the following, if true, could best be used by the highway official to support the official's proposal in the face of the taxpayer's objection?

options.a: The use of deicing salt causes corrosion of reinforcing steel in concrete bridge decks and damage to the concrete itself, thereby considerably shortening the useful life of concrete bridges.
options.b: Severe icing conditions can cause power outages and slow down the work of emergency crews trying to get power restored.
options.c: In weather conditions conducive to icing, ice generally forms on the concrete surfaces of bridges well before it forms on parts of the roadway that go over solid ground.
options.d: Aside from its potential use for deicing bridges, ECC might also be an effective means of keeping other concrete structures such as parking garages and airport runways ice free.
options.e: If ECC were to be used for a bridge surface, the electric current would be turned on only at times at which ice was likely to form.

questions.stimulus_text: In virtually any industry, technological improvements increase labor productivity, which is the output of goods and services per person-hour worked. In Parland's industries, labor productivity is significantly higher than it is in Vergia's industries. Clearly, therefore, Parland's industries must, on the whole, be further advanced technologically than Vergia's are.
questions.question_text: The argument is most vulnerable to which of the following criticisms?

options.a: It offers a conclusion that is no more than a paraphrase of one of the pieces of information provided in its support.
options.b: It presents as evidence in support of a claim information that is inconsistent with other evidence presented in support of the same claim.
options.c: It takes one possible cause of a condition to be the actual cause of that condition without considering any other possible causes.
options.d: It takes a condition to be the effect of something that happened only after the condition already existed.
options.e: It makes a distinction that presupposes the truth of the conclusion that is to be established.

questions.stimulus_text: Chaco Canyon, a settlement of the ancient Anasazi culture in North America, had massive buildings. It must have been a major cultural center. Analysis of wood samples shows that some of the timber for the buildings came from the Chuska and San Mateo mountains, 50 miles from Chaco Canyon. Only a major cultural center would have the organizational power to import timber from 50 miles away.
questions.question_text: In the argument given, the two portions in boldface play which of the following roles?

options.a: The first is a premise used to support the argument's main conclusion; the second is the argument's main conclusion.
options.b: The first is the argument's main conclusion; the second is a premise used to support that conclusion.
options.c: The first is one of two premises used to support the argument's main conclusion; the second is the other of those two premises.
options.d: The first is a premise used to support the argument's main conclusion; the second is a premise used to support another conclusion drawn in the argument.
options.e: The first is inferred from another statement in the argument; the second is inferred from the first.

questions.stimulus_text: Paper and plastic grocery bags are a continuing problem for the city, both as litter and in landfills. To discourage their use, the city council has proposed a tax on each bag to be paid by the supermarkets that supply them. Several environmental groups, however, oppose the tax despite having brought the issue to the council's attention in the first place.
questions.question_text: Which of the following, if true, would best explain the environmental groups' opposition to the proposed tax?

options.a: The city's current tax revenue is insufficient to pay the cost of recycling paper and plastic grocery bags.
options.b: The cost to the city of collecting and disposing of a grocery bag is greater than the proposed tax per bag.
options.c: Plastic bags account for 90 percent of the grocery bags that litter the city's streets and take up space in landfills.
options.d: Only supermarkets distributing more than a certain number of grocery bags each month would be subject to the proposed tax.
options.e: Supermarkets plan to pass the tax on to consumers, who are unlikely to change their behavior based on the small increase in their grocery bills.

questions.stimulus_text: Regulations will not allow a pesticide that is toxic to humans to be used inside houses unless the pesticide will dissipate completely from the air within eight hours after its application. One test pesticide manufacturers typically use to determine how quickly anti-termite pesticides dissipate involves spraying the pesticides on the walls of room-sized plywood boxes and then timing their dissipation.
questions.question_text: Which of the following would it be most useful to know in order to evaluate whether a dissipation time of just under eight hours on the manufacturers' test indicates an anti-termite pesticide that is toxic to humans obeys regulations for use in houses?

options.a: Whether anti-termite pesticides dissipate more slowly in furnished rooms than in plywood boxes
options.b: Whether people who apply anti-termite pesticides typically wear protective equipment that prevents them from being exposed to the pesticide
options.c: Whether people whose house is being treated with anti-termite pesticide generally know they should remain out of their house during the hours immediately after the pesticide's application
options.d: Whether there are anti-termite pesticides toxic to humans that, when subjected to the manufacturers' test, dissipate completely from the air in the boxes in well under eight hours
options.e: Whether anti-termite pesticides that are not toxic to humans tend to take longer to dissipate than those that are toxic

questions.stimulus_text:
In a typical year, Innovair's airplanes are involved in 35 collisions while parked or being towed in airports, with a resulting yearly cost of $1,000,000 for repairs.

To reduce the frequency of ground collisions, Innovair will begin giving its ground crews additional training, at an annual cost of $500,000. Although this will cut the number of ground collisions by about half at best, the drop in repair costs can be expected to be much greater, since
questions.question_text: Which of the following most logically completes the argument?

options.a: most ground collisions happen when ground crews are rushing to minimize the time a delayed airplane spends on the ground
options.b: a ground collision typically occurs when there are no passengers on the airplane
options.c: the additional training will focus on helping ground crews avoid those kinds of ground collisions that cause the most costly damage
options.d: the $500,000 cost figure for the additional training of ground crews includes the wages that those crews will earn during the time spent in actual training
options.e: most ground collisions have been caused by the least experienced ground-crew members

questions.stimulus_text: Many agriculturally intensive areas of the world are beginning to encounter water scarcity problems. As a result, many farmers in these areas are likely to reduce their output as the water supply they need in order to maintain production shrinks. However, one group of farmers in such a region plans to increase their production by implementing techniques for water conservation.
questions.question_text: Which of the following, if true, would most strongly support the prediction that the group's plan will succeed?

options.a: Farmers that can gain a larger share of the food market in their regions will be better positioned to control more water resources.
options.b: Most agricultural practices in areas with water shortages are water-intensive.
options.c: Other regions of the world not facing water shortages are likely to make up for the reduction in agricultural output.
options.d: Demand for agricultural products in the group's region is not expected to decline.
options.e: More than half the water used for agriculture in the farmers' region is lost to evaporation or leakage from irrigation channels.

questions.stimulus_text: Scientists propose placing seismic stations on the floor of the Pacific Ocean to warn threatened coastal communities on the northwestern coast of the United States of approaching tidal waves caused by earthquakes. Since forewarned communities could take steps to evacuate, many of the injuries and deaths that would otherwise occur could be avoided if the government would implement this proposal.
questions.question_text: The answer to which of the following questions would be most important in determining whether implementing the proposal would be likely to achieve the desired result?

options.a: When was the last time that the coastal communities were threatened by an approaching tidal wave?
options.b: How far below sea level would the stations be located?
options.c: Would there be enough time after receiving warning of an approaching tidal wave for communities to evacuate safely?
options.d: How soon after a tidal wave hits land is it safe for evacuees to return to their communities?
options.e: Can the stations be equipped to collect and relay information about phenomena other than tidal waves caused by earthquakes?

questions.stimulus_text: Because the cork stoppers in wine bottles can leak, crumble, or become moldy, winemakers must often discard a significant proportion of their inventory of bottled wine. Bottlemaster, Inc., produces a plastic stopper that cannot leak, crumble, or mold. Bottlemaster's plastic stopper is available to winemakers for a price only slightly higher than that of traditional cork stoppers, and cork prices are expected to rise considerably in the near future. Therefore, Bottlemaster's plastic stopper will increase its share of the market for wine-bottle stoppers.
questions.question_text: In the argument given, the two portions in boldface play which of the following roles?

options.a: The first and the second each provide evidence to support the conclusion of the argument.
options.b: The first presents information that, in light of other information presented, provides evidence to support the conclusion of the argument; the second is that conclusion.
options.c: The first is evidence that has been used to support a position the argument opposes; the second is the conclusion of the argument.
options.d: The first is evidence that has been used to support a position that the argument opposes; the second provides evidence to support the conclusion of the argument.
options.e: The first is a claim the argument challenges; the second is a conclusion on which that challenge is based.

questions.stimulus_text: Only a reduction of 10 percent in the number of scheduled flights using Greentown's airport will allow the delays that are so common there to be avoided. Hevelia airstrip, 40 miles away, would, if upgraded and expanded, be an attractive alternative for fully 20 percent of the passengers using Greentown airport. Nevertheless, experts reject the claim that turning Hevelia into a full-service airport would end chronic delays at Greentown.
questions.question_text: Which of the following, if true, most helps to justify the experts' position?

options.a: Turning Hevelia into a full-service airport would require not only substantial construction at the airport itself but also the construction of new access highways.
options.b: A second largely undeveloped airstrip close to Greentown airport would be a more attractive alternative than Hevelia for many passengers who now use Greentown.
options.c: Hevelia airstrip lies in a relatively undeveloped area but would, if it became a full-service airport, be a magnet for commercial and residential development.
options.d: If an airplane has to wait to land, the extra jet fuel required adds significantly to the airline's costs.
options.e: Several airlines use Greentown as a regional hub, so that most flights landing at Greentown have many passengers who then take different flights to reach their final destinations.

questions.stimulus_text: Farmer: Worldwide, just three grain crops—rice, wheat, and corn—account for most human caloric intake. To maintain this level of caloric intake and also keep pace with global population growth, yields per acre from each of these crops must have to increase at least 1.5 percent every year, given that the supply of cultivated land is diminishing. Therefore, the government should increase funding for research into new ways to improve yields.
questions.question_text: Which of the following is an assumption on which the farmer's argument depends?

options.a: It is solely the government's responsibility to ensure that the amount of rice, wheat, and corn produced worldwide keeps pace with global population growth.
options.b: Increasing government funding for research into new ways to improve the yields per acre of rice, wheat, and corn crops will help to increase total worldwide annual production of food from these crops.
options.c: Increasing the yields per acre of rice, wheat, and corn is more important than increasing the yields per acre of other crops.
options.d: Current levels of funding for research into ways of improving grain crop yields per acre have enabled grain crop yields per acre to increase by more than 1.5 percent per year worldwide.
options.e: In coming decades, rice, wheat, and corn will become a minor part of human caloric intake, unless there is government-funded research to increase their yields per acre.
